gpt55_review_summary: Maxwell the 3rd reviewed the public UX diff and found three issues: public deliverables were untracked, the installed-wheel quickstart referenced a file that would not be packaged, and maintainer-only CLI classification was incomplete. The branch now tracks the new files, exposes python -m cachet.quickstart_local as a packaged quickstart, keeps examples/quickstart_local.py as a source wrapper, verifies the built wheel quickstart in a fresh venv, and documents all installed Cachet command families plus document-kv compatibility aliases under docs/release-ops.
scope: README.md; docs/getting-started.md; docs/concepts.md; docs/production.md; examples/quickstart_local.py; src/cachet/quickstart_local.py
verification: PYTHONPATH=src python -m cachet.quickstart_local -> generated chunks: 4; materialized bytes: 340; engine handle: document-kv://req-local
python examples/quickstart_local.py -> generated chunks: 4; materialized bytes: 340; engine handle: document-kv://req-local

poetry build -f wheel; install dist/cachet_kv-0.2.0-py3-none-any.whl in a fresh venv; python -m cachet.quickstart_local -> generated chunks: 4; materialized bytes: 340; engine handle: document-kv://req-local
root README forbidden-term scan for PR evidence, GPT-5.5, branch protection, release bundle, and Databricks before advanced links -> no matches
changed-file secret scan for Databricks, GitHub, OpenAI, LangSmith, and private-key patterns -> no matches
Maxwell the 3rd reviewer found untracked public deliverables, an installed-wheel quickstart gap, and incomplete CLI audience classification; all findings were resolved
what_changed: Rewrote the root README into a short public Cachet landing page with install, audience fit, architecture flow, no-cloud quickstart, stable API surface, and next-step links.
Added beginner docs, concepts glossary, production guidance, issue templates, roadmap, changelog, release notes, and external-friendly contributing guidance.
Moved maintainer evidence and historical root README material under docs/release-ops while keeping human benchmark summaries under benchmarks/current and archiving failed SGLang run folders.
Added a packaged python -m cachet.quickstart_local local example and source-checkout wrapper so installed-wheel users have a real runnable quickstart.
Documented stable user commands separately from maintainer-only benchmark, Databricks, probe, smoke, release, governance, and compatibility CLI aliases.
why: Make a clean-room user understand Cachet, install it, run a local example, and choose the right next document without needing Databricks, release evidence, PR sidecars, or benchmark/audit context.
